value_chain_position: Engineering design and simulation platform layer — owns the dominant CAD/CAM/BIM toolchain (AutoCAD, Revit, Fusion 360, Construction Cloud) used to design physical products, buildings, and factories; increasingly integrating digital twin, generative design, and AI foundation models for manufacturing and construction. A workflow monopoly in AEC and a strong #2/#3 in manufacturing CAD.
gm_trajectory: EXPANDING/STABLE at the top — gross margin held at ~91% (non-GAAP ~92%) across FY24→FY25→FY26 on +12%→+17.5% revenue, i.e. zero compression while scaling. This is the moat lie-detector reading positive: pricing power and switching costs intact, NOT commoditization, despite Siemens/Dassault/PTC competition.
bull_thesis: ADSK is a 91%-gross-margin workflow monopoly being handed to you at 17.7x FCF after a ~30% de-rating to ~$198 (near 52-wk low of $204.80), at a moment when its true earning power is INFLECTING, not stalling. (1) MOAT: gross margin held flat at ~91% on +17.5% revenue — the single cleanest pricing-power signal available; Revit/AutoCAD/Construction Cloud are the system of record for AEC with brutal switching costs (firms standardize entire global delivery ecosystems on it — 'sixth EBA, second largest deal in Autodesk history,' FY26 Q1). (2) THEME DURABILITY: AECO (50% of revenue) is riding multi-year secular demand — 'sustained investment in data centers, infrastructure, and industrial buildings more than offsetting softness in commercial' (FY26 Q2/Q3); web-confirmed U.S. construction backlog at 8.8 months (Apr 2026, multi-year high) with data centers heading to ~40% of office construction by 2028. This is not a capex air-pocket — it is a structural build-out. (3) EARNINGS INFLECTION masked by noise: headline FY26 GAAP EPS looks flat (+2.1%) but is crushed by one-time restructuring ($105M) + SBC catch-up ($54M); TTM diluted EPS is 6.86, latest-Q EPS +231% YoY, and web-confirmed FY27 guidance is $12.29–$12.56 non-GAAP (+18–20%) on $8.10–$8.17B revenue (+12–13%). (4) MARGIN EXPANSION with an activist on the board: management committed to ~45% underlying op margin by FY29 (~900 bps lift), directly validating Starboard Value's public proxy thesis — a credible, dated, accountable program already showing up (Q2/Q3 op margins 39%/38%). (5) CAPITAL RETURN: buyback +50% to ~$1.3B funded from net cash (~$200M), repurchasing at $280–306 avg — ABOVE today's price. 100% beat rate (7/7), reliability High. Conditions to hold: AECO demand stays durable, underlying revenue stays ~11–12% c/c, and the FY29 margin walk delivers.
bear_thesis: The bear case is about the GROWTH RATE decelerating and the multiple being a value trap, not about the cash flows breaking. (1) NEW-BUSINESS SOFTNESS: management repeatedly concedes 'headwinds to new business growth' — the strength is renewals, EBAs, and new-transaction-model mechanics, NOT new-logo acquisition. The secular-disruptor framing oversells what is increasingly a mature, optimizing annuity. (2) OPTICAL DECELERATION becomes real: billings/FCF tailwinds from the annual-billing transition 'significantly diminish next year' (FY26 Q3); FY27 reported growth steps down and a skeptical market may not separate mechanical from fundamental, keeping the multiple depressed for quarters. (3) AI MONETIZATION IS NARRATIVE, NOT DOLLARS: 'will play out over time' (Q3) — the robotics_automation/generative-design tag rests on Fusion 360 optionality that has not shown up in segment revenue; if AI compresses the value of CAD seats faster than ADSK monetizes AI, the moat erodes. (4) VALUE-TRAP RISK: 17.7x FCF can de-rate further if growth settles at ~10% and margin gains are spent re-investing in the AI roadmap rather than dropping to FCF; Starboard's 45% target could force under-investment. (5) WEAK INSIDER CONVICTION: open-market 4 buys (5,489 sh) vs 22 sells (52,832 sh) — no insider is backing the truck up at $198. (6) THE PARTS DON'T trade at a premium if the theme PAUSES: a 12–18 month construction/data-center capex air-pocket would expose AECO's cyclicality and compress the whole-company multiple toward the low end of the peer band. Confirmation: underlying c/c revenue dropping below ~9%, gross margin finally compressing, or FY29 margin walk slipping.
sop_bull: Favorable segment Sum-of-Parts (FY26 segment revenue x premium peer EV/Sales for a 91%-GM franchise; ~213M diluted shares; +$0.20B net cash). AECO $3.58B x 11.0x = $39.4B (fastest-growing, most durable, data-center/infra-levered, Bentley/infra-software premium). AutoCAD family $1.79B x 9.0x = $16.1B (mature but ~monopoly stickiness, ultra-high margin). Manufacturing $1.38B x 9.5x = $13.1B (Fusion 360 + generative/robotics optionality, Dassault/PTC comp). M&E $0.33B x 6.0x = $2.0B. Other $0.125B x 5.0x = $0.6B. Segment EV = $71.2B; + net cash $0.2B = equity $71.4B / 213M sh = ~$335/share.
sop_bear: Adverse Sum-of-Parts ASSUMING THE THEME PAUSES 12–18 MONTHS (data-center/infra capex air-pocket; multiples compress to the low end of the peer band; AECO de-rated for cyclicality). AECO $3.58B x 6.5x = $23.3B. AutoCAD $1.79B x 6.5x = $11.6B (sticky, holds best). Manufacturing $1.38B x 6.0x = $8.3B (AI/generative optionality discounted to zero). M&E $0.33B x 4.0x = $1.3B. Other $0.125B x 3.5x = $0.4B. Segment EV = $44.9B; + net cash $0.2B = equity $45.1B / 213M sh = ~$212/share — i.e. even a full theme-pause SoP lands roughly at today's ~$198–205 price, which is the asymmetry: the market is already pricing the pause.
sop_breakdown: Base-case segment SoP at mid-cycle peer EV/Sales for a 91%-GM software compounder: AECO $3.58B x 8.75x = $31.3B; AutoCAD family $1.79B x 7.75x = $13.9B; Manufacturing $1.38B x 7.75x = $10.7B; M&E $0.33B x 5.0x = $1.7B; Other $0.125B x 4.0x = $0.5B. Segment EV = $58.1B; + net cash $0.2B = equity $58.3B / ~213M diluted sh = ~$274/share, rounded to a $270 base ($255–$285 band). Cross-check vs whole-company: TTM FCF $2.73B / FY27 guide $2.7–2.8B at a 21x FCF multiple (still a discount to Dassault/Bentley high-margin peers) = ~$57–59B EV → ~$270/sh — the FCF and segment approaches converge. The current $198 price implies ~14.5x FY27 FCF, a multiple consistent with a no-growth utility, which the +12–13% revenue / +18–20% EPS guide contradicts.
risk_reward: Downside-to-break ~$212 (adverse theme-pause SoP, ~+7% above today's $198) vs upside-to-fair ~$270 (+36%). Because the bear-case SoP sits ABOVE the current price, the asymmetry is favorable — roughly 5:1 (upside ~$72 vs realistic downside ~$30–40 to a true overshoot in the $160s on a hard recession). The market is already discounting a theme pause that the construction-backlog data refutes.
catalyst_status: FIRED (for the record only; does NOT drive this profitable-disruptor verdict). The load-bearing dated events in-window are spent: FY26 Q4 already printed (2026-02-26, web-confirmed: rev $1.96B +19%, non-GAAP EPS $2.85 beat) and FY27 guidance is out ($8.10–8.17B rev, $12.29–12.56 EPS); the Oct-7-2025 Investor Day and the FY29 45%-margin target are public; the Starboard proxy campaign is resolved into a board refresh + margin commitment. There is no pending binding catalyst — this is judged on theme durability, moat, and reinvestment economics, where it scores well, NOT on an imminent re-rate trigger.
peer_comps_note: theme_map true_competitors: PTC, Dassault (DSY.PA), Hexagon (HEXA-B.ST), Siemens (SIE.DE), MSFT, Bentley (BSY). ADSK's 91% gross margin and ~33–36% FCF margin are superior to PTC and BSY per the theme_map; high-margin design-software peers (Dassault, Bentley) carry ~9–13x EV/Sales, anchoring the premium leg of the SoP. The base-case ~7.75–8.75x blended multiple is a DISCOUNT to that band — conservative given ADSK's monopoly position in AEC. Sanity check passes: the SoP does not require ADSK to out-multiple its own peers, only to close part of the gap to them.
consensus_delta: Wall Street is mis-reading a MECHANICAL deceleration as a FUNDAMENTAL one. (1) The street fixates on the optically flat FY26 GAAP EPS (+2.1%) and the stepping-down FY27 reported growth rates without normalizing for the one-time restructuring ($105M) + SBC catch-up ($54M) charges and the lapping of the annual-billing/new-transaction-model tailwinds that management explicitly pre-warned 'significantly diminish next year.' Underlying c/c revenue is steady at ~11–12% and TTM EPS is 6.86 vs the 5.23 fiscal-year figure the screen anchors to. (2) Analyst-target velocity is -11% and the stock is at 3% of its 52-wk range — sentiment is washed out, yet the FY27 guide (web-confirmed +12–13% rev, +18–20% EPS) and the activist-validated FY29 45% margin walk are NOT priced. (3) The street treats AECO as cyclical/at-risk; the load-bearing fact (web-verified Apr-2026 construction backlog 8.8 months, data centers → ~40% of office construction by 2028) says the largest segment is riding a multi-year secular build, not a capex air-pocket. The incorrect consensus assumption is 'Autodesk is a mature ~10% grower that has lost its premium' — the numbers show a 91%-GM franchise inflecting earnings with margin upside still ahead.
valley_of_death: Near-term (3–9 month) risks are real but bounded — there is no cash-burn hump or maturity wall (net CASH ~$200M, TTM FCF $2.73B, P/FCF 17.7x). The hazards are optical and sentiment-driven: (1) FY27 reported billings/FCF growth rates DECELERATE as the annual-billing transition laps — management flagged 'toughest new transaction model comparisons' in Q4 and that tailwinds 'significantly diminish next year' — so the next 1–2 prints will SHOW slower headline growth that a skeptical tape may sell, even though underlying growth is intact. (2) Macro/commercial-construction softness could deepen if rates stay high, pressuring the non-data-center parts of AECO. (3) AI-monetization disappointment: management's 'will play out over time' leaves a gap the market could punish if a competitor (or generic AI) appears to commoditize CAD seats. (4) The stock is already -30% YTD near 52-wk lows with negative target velocity — momentum/CTA selling can overshoot to the $160s before fundamentals reassert. None of these impair intrinsic value; they are entry-timing hazards.
positioning_washout: No dividend/yield base to force-liquidate (ADSK pays no dividend), so there is no mechanical income-fund exodus. The washout is GROWTH/MOMENTUM-driven: the ~30% YTD drawdown to the bottom 3% of the 52-wk range, -11% analyst-target velocity, and weak smart-money score (36/100) indicate growth and momentum funds have already been de-risking. Open-market insiders are net sellers (4 buys/5,489 sh vs 22 sells/52,832 sh) — no insider capitulation-buying signal yet. Institutional flow is neutral-to-positive (60/100). The setup is a partially-completed washout: the marginal momentum seller is largely out, but a final flush on a decelerating FY27 print is plausible — which is precisely why this is a 'B / watch for capitulation' rather than an 'A / aggressive entry.'
forcing_function: There is no single hard binary catalyst (the load-bearing dated events have FIRED). The re-rating forcing functions are sequential proof-points: (1) the NEXT 1–2 earnings prints (FY27 Q1 ~late-May 2026, FY27 Q2 ~Aug 2026) where the market must digest decelerating REPORTED growth and judge whether UNDERLYING c/c growth (~11–12%) and margin expansion hold — a clean separation of mechanical-vs-fundamental would force the multiple back up; (2) tangible evidence the FY29 45%-underlying-margin walk is on track (each quarterly op-margin print is a milestone); (3) any escalation or settlement from Starboard Value that hard-codes the margin program or board control. The cleanest entry trigger to WAIT for: the first post-FY26 print where reported growth optically decelerates AND the stock flushes — buy the proof that underlying growth + margins held while the headline scared the tape.
moderator_conclusion: ADSK is the textbook PROFITABLE-SECULAR-DISRUPTOR setup judged on the right axes. THEME DURABILITY: real and multi-year — AECO (50% of revenue) rides a structural data-center/infrastructure build (backlog at multi-year highs, web-confirmed), not a capex air-pocket. LOAD-BEARING POSITION: high (4/5) — Revit/AutoCAD/Construction Cloud are the AEC system of record; competitors can nibble manufacturing CAD (Dassault/PTC/Siemens) but cannot route around ADSK in architecture/construction without firms ripping out their entire global delivery toolchain. MOAT/lie-detector: PASSES decisively — gross margin held at ~91% on +17.5% revenue (expanding/stable, not compressing) = pricing power, not commoditization. REINVESTMENT economics: ROE 51.7%, ROIC 15.9%, FCF margin 33%+, with a credible activist-validated path to ~45% underlying op margin by FY29 = high incremental returns with TAM headroom in AI/Make. The SoP base case is ~$270 ($255–$285) vs ~$198 today (+36%), and — critically — even the ADVERSE theme-pause SoP (~$212) sits ABOVE the current price, so the market is already paying for a pause that the data refutes; risk/reward is ~5:1. The two scores diverge correctly: this is NOT an aggressive-entry name because the catalyst has FIRED and a final momentum flush on a decelerating-but-mechanical FY27 print is plausible — hence VERDICT B (watch for capitulation), conviction 4. But the VALUE case stands on its own legs — high-quality franchise, clean forensics (100% beat rate, credibility 4), 17.7x FCF, SoP discount — so value_conviction is also 4, ignoring the spent catalyst. Wait for the post-print flush; buy the proof that underlying growth and margins held while the headline scared the tape.
